message:
feat(search-core): add frankensearch RRF fusion bridge and expand search conformance tests

Integrate frankensearch's RRF fusion engine as an optional backend
behind the "semantic" feature flag. When enabled, the fusion module
builds lexical and semantic hit vectors from prepared candidates,
delegates to frankensearch's fs_rrf_fuse(), and maps results back
to the Agent Mail FusedResult type — ensuring identical ranking
behavior between standalone and integrated search paths.

- fusion.rs: Add build_fs_lexical_hits(), build_fs_semantic_hits(),
  and frankensearch-bridge fuse_rrf path (~240 new lines) with
  doc_id round-trip mapping and deterministic tie-breaking
- query_integration.rs: Expand query integration test assertions
- search_v3_conformance.rs: Expand v3 search conformance checks
- Cargo.lock: Add new transitive dependency
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"br-1xt0m","title":"[epic] TUI V3: Cinematic Showcase — Every Frankentui Feature, One Reference Application","description":"Transform the Agent Mail TUI from a functional operations console into THE reference application
that demonstrates every frankentui capability at production quality. This is not incremental
improvement — it is a generational leap that makes this TUI the thing people show off when they
want to prove what terminal UIs can do.

STRATEGIC CONTEXT:
- frankentui is a world-class TUI framework with 40+ widget types, charts, canvas drawing,
  text effects, markdown rendering, Mermaid diagrams, visual FX, clipboard, export, forms,
  drag-and-drop, tree widgets, log viewer, inspector overlay, error boundaries, and more.
- The Agent Mail TUI currently uses only ~5% of frankentui's capabilities: Sparkline,
  ProgressBar, CommandPalette, NotificationQueue, VirtualizedList, and heatmap_gradient.
- 95% of frankentui's power is sitting unused. This epic systematically integrates ALL of it.

WHAT ALREADY EXISTS (br-2bbt delivered):
- 14 screens: Dashboard, Messages, Threads, Agents, Search, Reservations, ToolMetrics,
  SystemHealth, Timeline, Projects, Contacts, Explorer, Analytics, Attachments
- CommandPalette with ~160 entries + HintRanker + BayesianScorer
- NotificationQueue with event-to-toast mapping
- VirtualizedList on Messages, Timeline, Search, Explorer
- 8 custom widgets in tui_widgets.rs (BrailleActivity, MetricTile, etc.)
- Full theme system with TuiThemePalette (~70 semantic tokens)
- Dock layout system with responsive breakpoints
- Macro recording/playback engine

WHAT THIS EPIC ADDS (the remaining 95%):
Track 1: Advanced Data Visualization — LineChart, BarChart, Canvas/Braille real-time rendering
Track 2: Rich Document Rendering — GFM markdown, syntax highlighting, inline images
Track 3: Cinematic Text Effects — Gradients, glow, wave, pulse, ambient animations
Track 4: Mermaid Diagram Integration — Agent topology, thread flows, system architecture
Track 5: Tree Widget for Thread Hierarchies — 5 guide styles, expandable branches
Track 6: Log Viewer for Event Streaming — Filter/search/follow with severity coloring
Track 7: Clipboard & Screen Export — OSC 52 copy, HTML/SVG/Text export
Track 8: Focus Graph & Spatial Navigation — Arrow-key panel traversal, focus memory
Track 9: Form System for Data Entry — Compose messages, register agents, create reservations
Track 10: Ambient Visual FX Intelligence — System state encoded as visual atmosphere
Track 11: Inspector Overlay & Debug Mode — Widget tree debugger, performance profiler
Track 12: Error Boundaries & Graceful Degradation — Per-screen panic recovery
Track 13: Drag & Drop Operations — Move messages between threads, keyboard DnD
Track 14: Dynamic Theme Engine — 5 built-in themes, hot-switching, preview, persistence
Track 15: Comprehensive Testing & Quality Gates

DEPENDENCY STRATEGY:
- Tracks 1, 2, 3 are highest ROI and can proceed in parallel
- Track 4 (Mermaid) depends on Track 2 (rendering pipeline)
- Track 9 (Forms) depends on Track 8 (focus management)
- Track 13 (DnD) depends on Track 8 (focus management)
- Track 15 (Testing) depends on all implementation tracks

DEFINITION OF DONE:
- Every frankentui feature category has at least one production integration
- Frame render < 16ms at p95 on 200x50 terminal with live data
- All 14 screens visually enhanced (before/after documented)
- Zero hardcoded colors, zero raw border types, zero manual focus management
- Comprehensive test coverage: unit + snapshot + E2E + perf benchmarks","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All 15 tracks implemented and passing tests
- [ ] Every frankentui widget category used in at least one screen
- [ ] Frame render budget < 16ms at p95 maintained
- [ ] All existing functionality preserved (zero regressions)
- [ ] HTML/SVG export produces faithful screen captures
- [ ] Clipboard integration works across Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm, Ghostty
- [ ] 5 built-in themes all render correctly across all 14 screens
- [ ] Mermaid diagrams render in-terminal for agent topology
- [ ] Rich markdown with syntax highlighting in message preview
- [ ] Visual FX ambient layer encodes system health state

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:02.945941558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","showcase","tui","ux","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1xt0m","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:45.910203855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":581,"issue_id":"br-1xt0m","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ARCHITECTURAL CONTEXT (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

This epic exists because of a stark capability gap: frankentui provides 40+ widget categories spanning charts, canvas drawing, text effects, markdown rendering, Mermaid diagrams, visual FX, forms, drag-and-drop, tree widgets, log viewer, inspector overlay, error boundaries, clipboard, export, and 5 built-in themes — but the Agent Mail TUI uses only ~5% of this. Specifically, only Sparkline, ProgressBar, CommandPalette, NotificationQueue, VirtualizedList, and heatmap_gradient are currently integrated.

The strategic goal is to make this TUI THE showcase reference application for frankentui. When someone asks 'what can frankentui do?', the answer should be 'look at Agent Mail.' This serves the project because:
1. It demonstrates frankentui's production readiness in a real-world application
2. It makes the Agent Mail operations console genuinely best-in-class
3. It provides a comprehensive integration test surface for frankentui features
4. It creates a visual differentiator that makes Agent Mail memorable

RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING EPICS:
- br-3vwi (TUI V2): Nearly complete, delivered the 14-screen foundation
- br-2bbt (TUI V2 Showcase-Grade): Delivered command palette, toasts, virtualization, native charts, modals; 8 tracks still open (saved filters, batch selection, docs, network graph, archive browser, activity feed, compose panel, docs phase 2)
- br-1xt0m (THIS EPIC): Goes beyond br-2bbt into features that are not in any existing epic: Canvas/Braille visualization, Mermaid diagrams, text effects, ambient visual FX, log viewer, tree widget for threads, clipboard/export, forms system, inspector overlay, error boundaries, drag-and-drop, and 5-theme engine

No overlap with br-2bbt open tracks. br-2bbt.17 (Compose Panel) and br-1xt0m T9 (Form System) are related but distinct: br-2bbt.17 is about the compose UX pattern, T9 is about integrating frankentui's form widget system as the implementation technology.

IMPLEMENTATION PRIORITY ORDER:
P0 (highest ROI, no dependencies): T1 (Charts), T2 (Markdown), T5 (Tree Widget)
P1 (high value, some dependencies): T3 (Text Effects), T6 (Log Viewer), T7 (Clipboard/Export), T8 (Focus), T12 (Error Boundaries), T14 (Themes), T15 (Testing)

Changes made during plan-space review:

1. ADDED T0 (br-241rg): Feature gate verification prerequisite. Blocks all implementation tracks. Prevents compilation failures from missing feature gates.

2. FIXED T4.2 dependency: Removed incorrect dependency on T2.1 (markdown pipeline). Mermaid diagrams render to Canvas directly, not through the markdown pipeline. This was an articulation point risk that artificially serialized T4 behind T2.

3. ELEVATED T12 to P0: Error boundaries are foundation infrastructure, not nice-to-have. Every subsequent track benefits from panic recovery. Moving to P0 means it gets done early.

4. ADDED per-track test beads: T1.6, T2.4, T5.3, T6.3, T8.3, T10.2, T14.3 — each track now has dedicated unit tests with specific test counts, logging requirements, and acceptance criteria. This replaces the single catch-all T15.1 which was too vague.

5. ADDED subtasks to T11 (Inspector): T11.1 (implementation) + T11.2 (tests). Track was previously empty.

6. ADDED subtasks to T13 (DnD): T13.1 (mouse), T13.2 (keyboard), T13.3 (tests). Track was previously empty.

7. SPLIT E2E testing: T15.3 is now an orchestrator calling three focused E2E scripts: T15.4 (charts), T15.5 (rendering), T15.6 (interaction). Total target: 50+ assertions with comprehensive logging and diagnostic artifact capture.

8. UPDATED T15.1: Narrowed scope to only Mermaid generation tests and form validation tests (everything else now covered by per-track test beads).

-{"id":"br-1xt0m.1","title":"[track] T16: Showcase Parity Hardening — Visual + UX Deficiency Remediation (No Guided Tour)","description":"Objective: Close the concrete visual and UX gap between Agent Mail TUI and the frankentui demo showcase by remediating the audited deficiencies across navigation, interaction substrate, chrome hierarchy, overlays, motion, action integrity, screen-level readability, and discoverability.

Background and evidence baseline (audit snapshots):
- Stale screen count hints (`1-8`) in keymap/help vs real screen registry size (`tui_chrome.rs:360`, `tui_keymap.rs:36`, `tui_screens/mod.rs:56`, `tui_screens/mod.rs:98`).
- Action menu execution pathways currently toast/placeholders instead of fully wired execution (`tui_app.rs:802`, `tui_app.rs:824`).
- Transition rendering is primitive/text-heavy (`tui_app.rs:3291`).
- Screen-level UX density and hierarchy gaps in Messages/Threads/Search/Timeline/SystemHealth.
- Reference strengths from showcase: layered hit-region architecture, mouse dispatch, stronger shell hierarchy, richer overlay stack, adaptive status semantics (`/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs`, `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`).

Non-goals / explicit exclusions:
- Guided tour system is intentionally out of scope for this track.
- No backwards-compatibility shims; fix architecture directly.

Definition of done:
1. All listed deficiencies are mapped to implemented beads and verified by tests.
2. Interaction trust restored: every visible action is executable or explicitly unavailable with rationale.
3. Chrome and screen readability improved at 80/100/120/160 columns.
4. Keyboard/mouse discoverability and keymap docs are synchronized and regression-tested.
5. E2E + snapshot + performance checks pass with no dependency cycles.

Implementation approach:
- Phase A foundations: IA/navigation, hit regions, chrome hierarchy, action integrity.
- Phase B experience polish: overlays (without tour), motion, typography/color/density.
- Phase C screen passes: Dashboard, Messages/Threads, Search/Timeline, SystemHealth.
- Phase D quality gates: unit/snapshot/e2e/perf + before/after evidence bundle.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:20.725214919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:59:25.490005825Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","t16-parity","tui","ux","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:20.725214919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":629,"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Context for future maintainers:
This track is the direct implementation plan derived from a side-by-side audit of Agent Mail TUI vs /dp/frankentui ftui-demo-showcase. The biggest gaps were interaction architecture (mouse/hit regions), shell hierarchy clarity, and execution-trust violations in action flows.

Critical audit anchors:
- stale discoverability strings: tui_chrome.rs:360, tui_keymap.rs:36, tui_screens/mod.rs:56
- placeholder action execution: tui_app.rs:802, tui_app.rs:824
- primitive transition layer: tui_app.rs:3291

Design intent:
1) fix foundations first (T16.1/T16.2/T16.3/T16.6),
2) then apply polish and density reductions,
3) then screen-specific passes,
4) finish with quality gates + evidence.
Guided-tour work is explicitly excluded by user direction.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:16:06Z"},{"id":672,"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Priority rebalance (plan-space optimization): P0 is now restricted to true foundational blockers (navigation substrate, hit-region/dispatch core, chrome semantics, action execution integrity, and top-level quality-gate umbrella). All validation and screen-specific scopes remain fully in-plan but moved to P1 where they are dependency-gated, to improve execution focus without reducing scope.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:58:34Z"},{"id":673,"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution order after critical-path rebalance:\n1) T16.1.1 -> T16.1.3 (navigation truth + jump semantics)\n2) T16.2.1 -> T16.2.2 (hit-region and dispatch substrate)\n3) T16.3.2 (status semantics)\n4) T16.6.1 -> T16.6.2 (action execution integrity)\n5) then proceed through remaining P1 implementation streams and T16.13 detailed validation chain.\n\nThis preserves full scope while reducing P0 concurrency noise.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:59:25Z"}]}
